scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every practical list-endpoint read stream and every dialect-expressible CRUD mutation across the connector's declared core commercial surface (customers, suppliers, products, invoices, orders, quotes, and the reference/master-data collections they depend on). Accounting-ledger-internal resources with no independent top-level collection semantics useful to a data-sync connector (accounting-years/entries/periods, journals, vouchers, layouts, templates, employees, app-roles, projects, cost-types, contacts/delivery-locations as customer sub-resources) are excluded as out_of_scope, documented per-endpoint below rather than as a blanket bucket.

excluded.reason: single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the customers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
customer contact-person sub-resource; no independent top-level collection identity, and the customers stream's own record already carries the contacts list as a nested field

sub-resource create nested under a specific customerNumber path segment; the dialect's writes.json actions target one fixed collection path per action, not a per-parent-record nested sub-collection with no fan_out declared for it
customer delivery-location sub-resource; no independent top-level collection, nested under a specific customerNumber
per-customer invoice sub-listing; identical record shape to the invoices_booked stream, already covered by that stream (unfiltered by customer)
per-customer invoice sub-listing; identical record shape to the invoices_drafts stream, already covered by that stream (unfiltered by customer)
aggregate balance/turnover computation for a single customer, not a syncable object-list record
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the suppliers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the products list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the invoices_booked list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
returns a rendered PDF binary, not a JSON record this dialect's schema/write model can express
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the invoices_drafts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

draft invoice creation requires composing invoice line items (product/quantity/account references) with e-conomic's own totals/VAT computation contract, which this dialect has no line-item sub-array write shape for beyond a flat record body; a malformed line composition would silently diverge from what a real e-conomic client sends
same line-item composition gap as draft invoice creation above

draft invoice deletion; breadth left for a future wave once the paired create/update line-item gap above is closed (deleting a resource this connector cannot itself create is a narrower, lower-value mutation to add in isolation)
returns a booking-instructions template body used only as an intermediate step to construct a POST /invoices/booked request; not itself a syncable record

books a draft invoice into a final, legally-relevant accounting state using the booking-instructions template from GET /invoices/drafts/{n}/templates/booking-instructions as its request body; expressing this two-step compound sequence (template fetch + dependent POST) is beyond a single declarative writes.json action and would need a WriteHook, which Pass B scope for this connector does not introduce
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the orders_drafts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
draft order creation requires composing order line items (product/quantity references), the same line-item composition gap as draft invoices above
same line-item composition gap as draft order creation above
draft order deletion; breadth left for a future wave, mirroring the draft-invoice-delete narrowing above
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the orders_sent list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

moves a draft order to sent status by POSTing the full order document (including its line items) to this collection; expressing the draft-to-sent transition faithfully needs the same order-line-item body shape the dialect cannot compose (see draft order creation above), not merely a path change
multipart file attachment upload, not a JSON record this dialect's write body construction can express
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the quotes_drafts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
draft quote creation requires composing quote line items, the same line-item composition gap as draft invoices/orders above
same line-item composition gap as draft quote creation above
draft quote deletion; breadth left for a future wave, mirroring the draft-invoice/order-delete narrowing above
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the quotes_sent list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
moves a draft quote to sent status by POSTing the full quote document (including line items); same line-item body shape gap as orders/sent above
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the departments list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
department master-data creation; low practical write demand relative to the connector's core commercial write surface (customers/suppliers/products/units/payment-terms), left for a future wave
same low-priority master-data write narrowing as department creation above
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the payment_terms list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the units list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the vat_types list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the vat_zones list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the accounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

general-ledger entry line drill-down nested under a specific account and accounting year; genuinely accounting-internal detail beyond this connector's declared customers/suppliers/products/invoicing surface
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the customer_groups list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
per-group customer sub-listing; identical record shape to the customers stream, already covered by that stream (unfiltered by group)
single-item detail GET; identical record shape to the product_groups list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
supplier master-data grouping collection; no corresponding write requirement in this pass and lower practical read demand than the customer/product group equivalents already covered
general-ledger fiscal-year container resource; accounting-internal, not part of this connector's declared customers/suppliers/products/invoicing/master-data surface
single-item detail GET on the already out-of-scope accounting-years collection
raw general-ledger journal entry lines; accounting-internal, out of this connector's declared surface
fiscal period sub-resource of the already out-of-scope accounting-years collection
aggregate fiscal-year total computation, not a syncable object-list record
accounting journal (ledger book) metadata; accounting-internal, out of this connector's declared surface
raw journal entry lines nested under a specific journal; accounting-internal detail, out of scope
accounting voucher documents nested under a specific journal; accounting-internal, out of scope
HR/timesheet-adjacent resource with no relationship to this connector's declared customers/suppliers/products/invoicing surface
e-conomic app-permission/role metadata (integration-account administration), not business data this connector syncs
static ISO-4217 currency reference list; every record this connector reads already carries its own currency code inline, so a separate lookup stream adds no additional data
invoice/order print-layout template metadata; presentation configuration, not business data
reusable document template metadata; presentation configuration, not business data
time/project-tracking dimension resource with no relationship to this connector's declared customers/suppliers/products/invoicing surface
project cost-type reference dimension; only meaningful alongside the already out-of-scope projects resource
static payment-method reference list; no independent business-data value beyond what invoice/order records already carry inline
agreement/account self-identification metadata endpoint, not a syncable business-data record
